evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Validate the numbers. Every chart has a known-good ground truth. Own the semantic layer, the tests, the documentation, the lineage. Strong SQL including complex queries
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour: validation, testing, documentation, and lineage ownership dominate; no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: CFD trading platform; financial metrics; regulatory reporting (CySEC, MiFID II); KYC/AML signals
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: High: fintech/trading platform with explicit regulatory (CySEC/MiFID II/GDPR/DORA) and compliance (KYC/AML) language signals high domain risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: Finance; Risk; Operations; Product; Business Development
evidence.data_team_maturity: building and scaling our new data platform from the ground up; platform direction and priorities are already set
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Early: greenfield platform build with defined direction; team is being established; candidate helps shape foundation and processes.
evidence.jd_authorship: Own the full delivery cycle; Collect requirements directly from stakeholders; Investigate source systems; Build dbt models from raw/staging into marts; Build Superset dashboards; Validate the numbers; Document the work; Stakeholder acceptance
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: highly specific technical ownership (dbt models, Superset dashboards, validation logic, lineage) with concrete day-to-day context shows deep domain expertise.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Finance, Risk, Operations, Product, Business Development; trading platform metrics; regulatory reporting
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Finance: primary audience is financial/risk teams for trading metrics, regulatory reporting, and financial compliance.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own the full delivery cycle; Translate vague asks into precise specifications; Own the semantic layer, tests, documentation, lineage; You don't hand off and forget
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic: candidate sets direction for analytics foundation, owns complete delivery cycle, shapes platform decisions, and drives validation standards.
evidence.ai_role: Use AI-assisted development workflows for SQL generation, documentation, validation, and analytics engineering tasks; daily use of advanced AI tools; agentic flows, code generation, validation, documentation. Mandatory
evidence.ai_role_explanation: AI user: candidate expected to use generative AI tools daily (LLMs, agents) to accelerate SQL generation, documentation, and validation tasks.
evidence.testing_framing: Own the semantic layer, the tests, the documentation, the lineage; Implement appropriate dbt tests; Validate the numbers
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: data quality testing and validation owned as core responsibility; candidate accountable for correctness throughout pipeline.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Validate the numbers. Every chart has a known-good ground truth. You can defend any number on any dashboard; regulatory reporting (CySEC, MiFID II, GDPR, DORA); KYC, AML; End-to-end ownership; the numbers are yours until the stakeholder signs off
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: governance, compliance, validation, and regulatory language pervasive; fear is bad data reaching financial decision-makers and regulators.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: building and scaling our new data platform from the ground up; shape the analytics foundation; platform direction and priorities are already set
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Greenfield: explicit greenfield signal; team building platform from scratch; direction set but execution/foundation shaping is candidate's role.
evidence.loss_aversion: Validate the numbers. Every chart has a known-good ground truth. You can defend any number on any dashboard, with reasoning and reproducible queries. Knowledge of CySEC / MiFID II / GDPR / DORA.
